section.heading: Prohibition on certain cell phone voice communications

subsection.heading: Prohibition.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of Transportation shall issue regulations—

paragraph.content: to prohibit an individual on an aircraft from engaging in voice communications using a mobile communications device during a flight of that aircraft in scheduled passenger interstate or intrastate air transportation; and

paragraph.chapeau: that exempt from the prohibition described in paragraph (1) any—

subparagraph.content: member of the flight crew on duty on an aircraft;
flight attendant on duty on an aircraft; and
Federal law enforcement officer acting in an official capacity.

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the following definitions apply:

paragraph.heading: Flight.—
paragraph.content: The term “flight” means, with respect to an aircraft, the period beginning when the aircraft takes off and ending when the aircraft lands.

subparagraph.heading: In general.—
subparagraph.content: The term “mobile communications device” means any portable wireless telecommunications equipment utilized for the transmission or reception of voice data.

subparagraph.heading: Limitation.—
subparagraph.content: The term “mobile communications device” does not include a phone installed on an aircraft.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–254, div. B, title IV, § 403(a), Oct. 5, 2018, 132 Stat. 3328.)
